nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clearmont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Georges, Normand, Pierre, Armand, Edouard, Gaston, Ghislaine, Gilles, Henri, Herve, Jean Francois.

French and English (Middlesex, of Norman and French Huguenot origin): habitational name from any of various places in France called (Le) Clermont, from Old French clair, cler ‘bright, clear’ + mont ‘hill’, i.e. a hill that could be seen a long way off. Compare Clairmont .

English (Middlesex, of Norman and possibly also Huguenot origin): variant of Claremont, from the Old French female personal name Claremonde, supposedly from ancient Germanic Claremunda, a hybrid compound of Latin clarus ‘famous’ + ancient Germanic mund ‘protection’.

variants.name: Clairmont
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adelard, Aime, Antoine, Aurore, Fernand.
French: variant of Clermont and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Clairmont is very rare in France.
In some cases possibly also English: variant of Claremont or Clermont . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de Clearmont was born in 1123, in Clermont, Oise, Picardie, France, her father, Renaud de Clermont II, was 42 and her mother, Clemence de Bar, was 13. She had at least 2 sons with William Smith Baron of Carrington. She died in 1173, in Carrington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de Clearmont est née en 1123, à Clermont, Oise, Picardie, France, son père, Renaud de Clermont II, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Clemence de Bar, avait 13 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec William Smith Baron of Carrington. Elle est décédée en 1173, à Carrington, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne de Clearmont 1123 in Clermont, Oise, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Renaud de Clermont II 42 und ihre Mutter Clemence de Bar 13. Sie hatte mit William Smith Baron of Carrington mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1173 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Carrington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Clearmont è nata il nel 1123 nel Clermont, Oise, Picardie, Francia, sua padre, Renaud de Clermont II, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Clemence de Bar, aveva 13 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con William Smith Baron of Carrington. Morì nel 1173, nel Carrington, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni.
